summary: Julia Evans shares a curated list of blogs she enjoys reading, spanning topics from distributed systems and Postgres to game development and finance. She emphasizes her deliberate strategy of reading outside her comfort zone to expand her knowledge. The list highlights mostly women and underrepresented voices in tech, covering both technical and non-technical subjects. Each recommendation comes with specific post suggestions and genuine enthusiasm for what she's learned from each writer.
key_points: Deliberately reading blogs outside her area of expertise to broaden her knowledge
Highlights a diverse range of voices including many women in tech writing about systems programming, databases, data science, and community management
Values blogs that mix technical depth with personal reflection, like Camille Fournier's transition from systems programming to engineering management
Reads non-technical blogs (marketing, finance, game development) because insights often transfer across domains
money_quotes: I'm trying to read more blogs about things I don't know much about yet, to expandify my mind.
I basically want to be her when I grow up.
I have almost no experience with this stuff, and I've learned a ton from just following her on Twitter.
I don't understand everything, but I understand 10x more about her research than I would if I didn't read her blog.
This is on my list of 'things I read that don't really use right now but I am reading them just in case!'
key_insight: Deliberately reading blogs outside your expertise — even when you don't fully understand them — is a powerful way to expand your thinking and discover unexpected connections.
